stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Mira Lehr was an American multimedia artist.
  • She often explored themes of nature and ecology in her work.
  • A multimedia work combines two or more artistic mediums.
  • Lehr completed the installation A Walk in the Garden in 2017, using burned paper and acrylic.
  • She completed the installation High Water Mark in 2021, using ink and gunpowder.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two installations. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Mira Lehr’s installations A Walk in the Garden and High Water Mark both explore themes of nature and ecology.
Though both installations are by Lehr, A Walk in the Garden (2017) uses burned paper and acrylic, while High Water Mark (2021) uses ink and gunpowder.
Lehr created numerous multimedia installations exploring ecology, including A Walk in the Garden and High Water Mark.
A multimedia installation combines two or more artistic mediums, as Lehr’s work often did.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes a difference between the two installations, noting that one uses burned paper and acrylic while the other uses ink and gunpowder.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes a similarity between the two installations rather than a difference. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence makes a generalization about Lehr’s installations; it doesn’t emphasize a difference between A Walk in the Garden and High Water Mark. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence defines multimedia work and references Lehr generally; it doesn’t emphasize a difference between the two installations.
